signals.title: USD — Geopolitical Peace Optimism + Inflation Shock
signals.text: DXY at 98.02 testing 2.5-month lows as reports of an imminent end to the US-Iran war trigger a sharp unwind of the geopolitical risk premium. WTI Crude plummeted -6.97% on the news, alleviating immediate energy-driven inflation fears despite Fed's Goolsbee warning of a lingering inflationary shock. The combination of falling oil and peace optimism drives a record risk-on rally in equities (S&P 500 +1.46%), stripping the USD of its safe-haven bid. USD COT net short -3,219 (week ending Apr 28) suggests specs were already lean on USD length prior to this move.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4370 (implied) as NZD outperforms the G8 complex while CAD lags significantly. NZD strength is underpinned by a hawkish RBNZ bias (Hike bias, next meeting 27 May) and a +225bp carry advantage over the SNB, while CAD is pressured by the -6.97% collapse in crude oil prices. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 vs a hawkish CB bias creates a high-conviction short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ reaffirms the hike path, especially as CAD remains the weakest G8 performer.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7239 supported by a 4.35% RBA rate (HIKING) vs Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD), providing a +60bp AUD carry premium. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction, while retail sentiment is at an extreme 89% short. This institutional vs retail divergence at the 0.7240 level suggests retail is unsuccessfully fading the trend; a sustained break higher risks a retail capitulation squeeze toward 0.7300.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.34 as the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage faces increasing friction from verbal intervention risks. Treasury Secretary Bessent's scheduled visit to Tokyo next week to discuss the 'weak yen' introduces a policy-driven ceiling. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 remains crowded, but the 1.05σ break in the USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation suggests the pair is becoming more sensitive to intervention headlines than yield differentials. Watch 160.00 for aggressive BoJ action.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Equity Linkage
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1755 exhibiting a significant correlation break against EuroStoxx (corr=+0.716 vs norm=-0.150, z=+1.99σ). The pair is currently trading as a pure 'risk-on' proxy, rising in lockstep with European equities (+2.68%) rather than following traditional yield or DXY dynamics. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% maintains a -175bp carry deficit for the EUR, suggesting the move is flow-driven rather than fundamental. Watch for a mean reversion in correlation if equity momentum stalls.
